channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish in road, 23rd July
item.description:
Large amount of rubbish in the middle of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotton Hill, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Santo Remedio Waste, 23rd July
item.description:
Bin bags are frequently left on the street for too long because they are not collected on time, and there does not appear to be a suitable place for waste to be stored while awaiting collection. As a result, the bags are often ripped open, leaving rubbish scattered across the street, creating an unpleasant environment and a potential health hazard. A proper, designated waste storage area should be provided rather than using the side of the road as a collection point. Leaving waste on the pavement is unsightly, negatively affects the local area and residents, and attracts pests. This has become a recurring problem, and I ask the council to work with the business to ensure waste is stored appropriately and collected more regularly.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 136-148, Tooley Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle facing opposite way on one way street, abandoned two months., 23rd July
item.description:
Vehicle facing opposite way on one way street, abandoned for two months.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warneford Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Santo Remedio Waste, 23rd July
item.description:
Bin bags are frequently left on the street for too long because they are not collected on time, and there does not appear to be a suitable place for waste to be stored while awaiting collection. As a result, the bags are often ripped open, leaving rubbish scattered across the street, creating an unpleasant environment and a potential health hazard. A proper, designated waste storage area should be provided rather than using the side of the road as a collection point. Leaving waste on the pavement is unsightly, negatively affects the local area and residents, and attracts pests. This has become a recurring problem, and I ask the council to work with the business to ensure waste is stored appropriately and collected more regularly.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tooley Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tire left on the pavement, 23rd July
item.description:
Hi, the tire is still there. It was flagged as sorted but it's still there

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shrubland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Seesaw damage, 23rd July
item.description:
Broken seesaw/spring rocker at playground behind Finnewcane Drive, Bromley, BR5 4ED. The seesaw/spring rocker has been damaged and is currently cordoned off with temporary barriers, but it has been left in this state for some time. The equipment is broken and unusable, and the barriers themselves are now a bit of an eyesore and trip hazard in the middle of the playground. Please could this be repaired or removed, and the barriers cleared once resolved.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stanton Close, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous bolts protruding, 23rd July
item.description:
Someone has tampered with the park bench, removed the nuts, moved the bench, and left dangerous bolts exposed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blenheim Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin full, 23rd July
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Pauls Cray Road, Chislehurst, Greater London

item.title: Tree Roots and height, 23rd July
item.description:
There is a tree outside residents property that has got quite big . The roots are now lifting and cracking my 9 inch wide front wall . could this please be investigated
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hill View Drive, Falconwood, Greater London
